fields.description:
# S4 â€” RFC 7662 token-introspection verifier

## Why
Introspection completes the "validate against an external Authorization Server" story for providers
that issue **opaque** access tokens (or where instant revocation matters) â€” Keycloak, Okta, Auth0,
WorkOS all expose a standard `/introspect` endpoint. It is the pattern the SDK's own `simple-auth`
example demonstrates.

## What
Add an **`IntrospectionTokenVerifier`** to `src/rebar/_mcp_auth.py`, activated by including
`introspection` in the `mcp.auth_strategies` list (the strategy-selection config key **introduced by
S2**, this story's dependency â€” not a pre-existing seam), with flat config keys THIS story registers: `auth_introspection_endpoint`,
`auth_introspection_client_id`, `auth_introspection_client_secret_env` (NAME of an env var holding
the client secret â€” no plaintext secret in config), `auth_introspection_allow_private_host` (bool, default `false`), and
`auth_introspection_allow_missing_aud` (bool, default `false`). It does not take a per-verifier
audience â€” it validates against the single `auth_resource_server_url` (S2). It POSTs the token
(RFC 7662) with client authentication, accepts only `active: true`, and maps the response into an
`AccessToken` (all RFC 7662 response fields are OPTIONAL, so each has a defined fallback): `scope`
(a space-delimited string per Â§2.2) is split into the scopes list (absent â†’ empty list),
`exp`â†’`expires_at` (absent â†’ `None`, per Expiry below), `sub`â†’`subject` (absent â†’ `None`), and
`client_id`â†’`client_id` (absent â†’ falls back to `sub`, else `"unknown"`); and stamps `resource=auth_resource_server_url` so the composite
check passes. NOTE: the SDK's `IntrospectionTokenVerifier` is an **example, not an exported/hardened
class** â€” this story WRITES its own (adapting the example), it does not import it. Adds the `auth_introspection_*` fields to the `McpConfig` dataclass + the `_SECTIONS['mcp']` coercer
in `src/rebar/_config_schema.py`; these auto-derive the `REBAR_MCP_AUTH_INTROSPECTION_*` **env-var
names**, which are added to `MCP_ENV_VARS` (in `src/rebar/mcp_server.py`) and `server.json`;
`scripts/check_server_manifest.py` passes. (The generated `mcp-reference.md`/`env-vars.md` are
regenerated for the full key set by S7; S1 fixes the generators to iterate `MCP_ENV_VARS`.)

## Approach & rationale
Adapt the SDK `simple-auth` example (the blessed reference) and add the production hardening its
docstring says it lacks: client authentication on the introspection call, strict audience handling, and fail-closed transport errors. Reuse the
already-present `httpx`.

## Scope
The introspection verifier only, on the S2 seam. Subset of the epic. Depends on S2. Independent of
S3/S5/S6.

## Security
Boundary-crossing. Requirements:
- Call the introspection endpoint over authenticated TLS (`client_secret_basic`, secret from
  `auth_introspection_client_secret_env`), **HTTPS-only** (an `http://` endpoint is rejected at
  construction), a **bounded HTTP timeout (default connect 5s / read 10s)**. Private/link-local
endpoint hosts (RFC 1918, loopback,
  `169.254.0.0/16`) are **rejected by default**; an operator with a legitimate internal AS sets
  `auth_introspection_allow_private_host=true` to opt out.
- Accept only `active:true`. **Audience:** RFC 7662 Â§2.2 permits `aud` to be a JSON string OR an
  array of strings â€” the check passes if `auth_resource_server_url` equals the string form or is a
  member of the array form. If the AS omits `aud` (many do), the default is to **reject**
  (fail-closed); an operator whose AS scopes tokens to this resource by other means sets
  `auth_introspection_allow_missing_aud=true` to accept aud-absent tokens (documented risk in S7).
- **Fail-closed transport:** a non-200, timeout, or malformed response **raises** (composite treats
  as non-acceptance â†’ deny); it never yields acceptance.
- **No caching in v1:** every request re-introspects the token with the AS, giving instant
  revocation. A positive-response cache (hashed key, bounded TTL) is a documented follow-on
  optimization, deliberately out of scope here to avoid the revocation-delay tradeoff.
- **Expiry:** when the response includes `exp`, set `AccessToken.expires_at` from it (a real
  positive value). When `active:true` but `exp` is absent (RFC 7662 permits it), set
  `expires_at=None` â€” validity is then bounded by re-introspection (every request re-checks the AS in v1). Never emit
  `expires_at=0` (the SDK reads a falsy value as no-expiry while looking like a set value).
- Tokens/secrets never logged.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The `auth_introspection_*` config fields exist in `McpConfig`/`_SECTIONS['mcp']`
      (`src/rebar/_config_schema.py`); their derived `REBAR_MCP_AUTH_INTROSPECTION_*` env-var names are
      present in `MCP_ENV_VARS` (`src/rebar/mcp_server.py`) and `server.json`; and
      `scripts/check_server_manifest.py` passes (observable oracle: the manifest drift test is green
      and both files contain the new env-var names).
- [ ] Unit test (tier: unit against the real verifier; the irreducible boundary â€” the introspection
      HTTP call â€” is a stateful fake fed production-shaped RFC 7662 responses) asserts an `active:true`
      response with a matching `aud` and an `exp` â†’ an `AccessToken` with expected scopes,
      `resource==auth_resource_server_url`, and a positive `expires_at`; and that an `active:true`
      response OMITTING `exp` yields `expires_at=None` (never 0), with validity bounded by
      re-introspection (observable oracle: `expires_at is None`).
- [ ] Unit tests assert rejection for: `active:false` (returns None); a response OMITTING `aud` when
      `auth_introspection_allow_missing_aud=false` (fail-closed reject) â€” and acceptance of the same
      response when the key is `true`; a mismatched `aud`; and an `aud` supplied as an ARRAY that
      does/does not contain `auth_resource_server_url` (accept/reject respectively) â€” each a distinct
      negative control. Also assert `scope` given as a space-delimited string is split into the
      expected scopes list.
- [ ] Unit test asserts a non-200 / timeout / malformed response **raises** (â†’ composite
      non-acceptance/deny) and never yields acceptance, and that every request re-introspects with no
      caching in v1 (observable oracle: the stateful fake's request count increments once per request
      â€” two identical requests hit the fake twice).
- [ ] Unit test asserts construction-time guards: the introspection request carries
      `client_secret_basic` (secret sourced from `auth_introspection_client_secret_env`); an `http://`
      endpoint is rejected; and a private/link-local host is rejected by default and accepted only
      with `auth_introspection_allow_private_host=true` (observable oracle: the fake receives Basic
      auth; rejection at construction for the http/private cases).
- [ ] Integration test (tier: integration â€” an httpx `ASGITransport` client driving the server's
      Streamable-HTTP ASGI app, obtained from the configured FastMCP via its Streamable-HTTP
      entrypoint (resolved at implementation time), NOT `build_server()`'s return directly; the
      external RFC 7662 `/introspect` endpoint is the SAME stateful fake used in the unit tests,
      injected via the verifier's httpx client so NO real network call is made) asserts an
      introspection-validated token â†’ 200 end-to-end, and an `active:false`/absent token â†’ 401.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass.

## Compatibility
Additive verifier, opt-in via the `introspection` strategy. No change to other verifiers or stdio.
